judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports are strong and nearly equivalent, but Report 1 edges ahead because it correctly identifies that the three-dot PR diff re-includes the already-merged scoring/scorer.py changes, whereas Report 2 frames the file diff as clean and only catches the redundant commits in the commit list.

report_1.evidence: `main`. ... Critically, the branch this work depended on, `feat/weighted-scorer`, has already been integrated into `main` ... so `main` is now the correct, up-to-date integration point — not `feat/weighted-scorer`, which is stale and no longer the place new work should land on top of.

report_2.evidence: `main`. ... in this specific case the branch this PR was actually stacked on (`feat/weighted-scorer`) is no longer a live target at all

report_1.evidence: `feat/pairwise-metric` was checked out from `feat/weighted-scorer` ... its work has already landed on `main`, but via a squash-style merge ... Yet `git merge-base --is-ancestor ac9d505 main` reports "not an ancestor" ... 1971f08, "Refactor scorer for weighted metrics (#42)"

report_2.evidence: `feat/pairwise-metric` was branched from `feat/weighted-scorer`, not from `main`. ... `feat/weighted-scorer` (`ac9d505`) is not an ancestor of `origin/main` ... `main`'s tip commit `1971f08` — "Refactor scorer for weighted metrics (#42)" ... `feat/weighted-scorer` itself was never fast-forwarded or deleted — it just sits there looking alive while being functionally dead

report_1.evidence: `git diff --stat main...feat/pairwise-metric` (triple-dot / merge-base diff — the calculation GitHub uses for a PR ... `scoring/scorer.py` (10 insertions, 3 deletions ...) comes entirely from W1 (`d6fdbc3`) and W2 (`2d3029b`) — pure weighted-scorer work. ... git's merge-base for `feat/pairwise-metric` vs. `main` is still the pre-feature commit `65204ad`, and any merge-base-based diff (including what GitHub would show for a PR) walks back through and re-includes W1–W3's changes

run `git rebase --onto main feat/weighted-scorer feat/pairwise-metric` ... Re-verify the diff: after rebasing, re-run `git diff main...feat/pairwise-metric` and confirm it now matches the clean 5-line, 2-file diff

report_2.evidence: `git rebase --onto origin/main feat/weighted-scorer feat/pairwise-metric` ... Confirm the result: `git diff origin/main feat/pairwise-metric` should still show exactly the same 5-line, 2-file delta, and `git log origin/main..feat/pairwise-metric` should now show only `X1` and `X2`
